summary: Transaction Display Currency Fix - 100% SUCCESS. All 10 tests passed. Verified: crypto transactions (ETH, BTC) are now correctly converted to company's preferred fiat currency (GBP). Example: 0.00775986237 ETH → £10.98 GBP (implied rate ~£1,414/ETH), 0.00031447 BTC → £15.25 GBP (implied rate ~£48,494/BTC). The fix properly uses base_currency from transactions for conversion instead of hardcoding USD.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix in companyController.ts line 662-710: getTransactions now correctly builds conversionRates using actual base_currency (ETH, BTC) from transactions
currencyConvert.ts line 196-222: processSingleCurrency now tries CoinGecko first for crypto conversions (FastForex was silently returning wrong rates)
Response structure correct: display_amount (numeric), display_currency (GBP), amount_display object with display_value formatted as '£X.XX GBP'
currency_info in response includes code, symbol, display_format fields
context_for_next_testing_agent: Transaction display currency fix is working correctly. Company 38 has 4 crypto transactions (2 ETH, 2 BTC). The getTransactions endpoint returns display_amount properly converted to GBP using CoinGecko rates. ETH rate ~£1,414, BTC rate ~£48,494 (market rates as of Feb 2026).

tests_executed.test_03_response_has_currency_info: PASSED - currency_info has code, symbol, display_format
tests_executed.test_05_transactions_have_display_amount: PASSED - All transactions have numeric display_amount
tests_executed.test_07_crypto_transactions_have_non_zer: PASSED - All 4 crypto transactions have display_amount > 0

bug_fix_verified.previous_issue: Transaction amounts showing as crypto values (0.00775 ETH → £0.01 GBP) instead of correct fiat conversion
bug_fix_verified.root_cause: companyController.ts was hardcoding sourceCurrency='USD' in currencyConvert calls
bug_fix_verified.fix_applied: Now uses actual base_currency from each transaction (ETH, BTC). currencyConvert.ts tries CoinGecko first for crypto
